rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERMA L. ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] (2) The petition caption does not disclose all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] (Is this the appropriate manner for requesting relief pursuant to Elections Code Sect. 2150 and is that authority for the Court to take away voting rights?) (3) Not all financial institution statements were filed. (4) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (5) It appears that the bond could actually be reduced... Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please be careful when making a courtesy copy of a petition for the Court. The one that was received by the assigned examiner had pp. 4-9 of the attorney timesheets between Schedules C and D of the accounting.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/17/2023, pending a disposition of the related heirship petition. The following issues have not been addressed since the initial hearing on 12/12/2022: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No original statements of the bank accounts listed on Schedule F have been filed. See LRSF 14.34(D). 4) A copy of the petition must be served on the person who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. See Probate Code section 1252(a). 5) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 6) Please file a copy of the closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate real property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIO V. MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition for final distribution or report of status of administration with a request (petition) for additional time has been filed yet, as required by Probate Code Section 12200 and as ordered on 11/22/21 and despite the additional time granted due to the administrator's not claiming her Letters for almost four months after appointment. An administrator's attorney's "declaration" is NOT the administrator's petition. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL B. FLYNN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/2023. Please provide the following information seven days prior to the hearing: 1) The omnibus clause needs to set forth the beneficiary. Reference to the will appears insufficient. In this case, the clause will need to mirror two different dispositions. 2) Petition to set forth the current trustee(s) of the 1996 Trust. 3) Pursuant to PC sec. 10954(b)(4), written waivers of accounting as the trustee(s) of each trust. Also, acceptance of The name of the acting trustee of the 1990 Trust and 1996 Trust, respectively. Written waives of accounting on file are executed as individuals in absence of description of capacity as trustee. 4) An authority under which the personal rep has distributed the assets listed on Inventory and Appraisal to the allegedly lawful owners. Petition does not 5) Written statement from the prior attorney if she also waives the statutory compensation. She will need to be properly noticed, too. 6) A new proposed order reflecting the above supplemental information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SECOND RESTATEMENT OF THE CHESTER ALLAN ROAMAN REVOCABLE TRUST

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/23. Prior to review of the petition, venue must first be properly addressed. Petitioner alleges that San Francisco Superior Court "obtained jurisdiction over the Trust" when Settlor died at his home, which is not the correct basis for venue. A supplemental declaration verified by petitioner must be filed by 4/10/23 addressing Probate Code 17002 and 17005, attaching a copy of the 16061.7 notice.
